name: Pork and Peanut Stir-Fry
recipeIngredient: 3 tbsp Kikkoman Lite Soy Sauce; divided
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice pork into thin, narrow strips.
2. Combine 2 teaspoons each of cornstarch and lite soy sauce, ginger, and pork; let stand for 15 minutes.
3. In a separate bowl, combine the remaining 2 teaspoons cornstarch, 2 tbsp plus 1 tsp lite soy sauce, vinegar, garlic powder, and 1/2 cup water. Set aside.
4. Heat 1 tbsp oil in a hot wok or large skillet over high heat.
5. Add pork and stir-fry for 2 minutes; remove from the pan and set aside.
6. Heat the remaining 1 tbsp oil in the same pan.
7. Add onion and carrot; stir-fry for 2 minutes.
8. Add zucchini; stir-fry for 2 minutes.
9. Return the pork and soy sauce mixture to the pan. Cook, stirring, until the sauce boils and thickens.
10. Stir in peanuts.
Makes 2 to 3 servings
Source: The Art of Stir-Frying Made Easy with Kikkoman Sauces
